summary: A brief report on Diane Hendricks' speech at the 2024 Republican National Convention, where she shared her personal story of overcoming early hardships and the loss of her husband to build ABC Supply. She emphasized the importance of hard work, job creation in America, and her belief in the American Dream.

principles.statement: The American Dream is achievable through hard work and determination.
principles.rationale: She views her own trajectory from a teenage single mother without a college degree to a billionaire business owner as living proof that the system works for those who apply themselves.
principles.source_quote: I'm living proof the American dream is possible through hard work and determination

principles.statement: Creating jobs in the United States is the most important outcome of business success.
principles.rationale: While building the largest roofing distributor in the country was a massive achievement, she considers the domestic employment of over 22,000 Americans to be the greater accomplishment.
principles.source_quote: But what's even more important is we are creating these jobs right here in the United States of America.

principles.statement: When faced with devastating personal and professional loss, choose to fight rather than fold.
principles.rationale: After the sudden death of her husband and co-founder, she had to make a conscious choice about the future of her family and company, ultimately deciding to push forward rather than give up.
principles.source_quote: I had to decide whether to fight or fold. And I chose to fight.

principles.statement: Every life is a sacred gift.
principles.rationale: Reflecting on her unplanned pregnancy at age 17, she frames the challenge not as a setback, but through a lens of faith that guided her subsequent work ethic.
principles.source_quote: At the age of 17 I became pregnant. That was not part of my plan. But I truly believe that every life is a sacred gift from God

mental_models.name: Fight or Fold
mental_models.description: A binary decision-making lens used during moments of extreme crisis or loss. It forces a clear choice between giving up (folding) or taking on the burden to continue forward (fighting).
mental_models.example: Applied when her husband and co-founder Ken died in 2007, leaving her as the sole owner of ABC Supply and a single mother to their children.

signature_quotes.text: I'm living proof the American dream is possible through hard work and determination.
signature_quotes.context: Speaking to the crowd at the 2024 Republican National Convention about her path to success.

signature_quotes.text: I had to decide whether to fight or fold. And I chose to fight.
signature_quotes.context: Recalling the aftermath of her husband's death 16 years prior.

signature_quotes.text: To all of you aspiring entrepreneurs out there, if I can make it, you can make it.
signature_quotes.context: Offering encouragement to future business owners during her RNC speech.

signature_quotes.text: My success story as a teenager without a college degree was only possible because I was so blessed to be born in the United States of America.
signature_quotes.context: Attributing her business success to the foundational opportunities provided by the US.
